archetype_selection.rationale: The project combines schema-governed glossary/profile authority with a claimed execution runtime and external downstream proof.

alternatives.reason_not_selected: Semantic governance is central, but runtime replay was decisive to v1.4 and v1.5 release acceptance.
Controls are important, but the delivered package also makes runtime and artifact claims.

decisive_gaps: No complete v1.3 adversarial validator suite was supplied.
The packaged example summarizes prior observations rather than embedding every source ZIP.

metrics.rationale: v1.3 is comparatively strong for Goal and requirement coverage.
v1.3 is comparatively strong for Functional correctness.
v1.3 is comparatively strong for Contract and schema integrity.
v1.3 is comparatively strong for Semantic and data consistency.
v1.3 is comparatively strong for Inventory, reference, and identity integrity.
v1.3 is comparatively strong for Evidence and release traceability.
v1.3 is comparatively strong for Reproducibility and replayability.
v1.3 is comparatively strong for Validator and resilience quality.
v1.3 is comparatively strong for Baseline regression and drift.
v1.3 is comparatively strong for Packaging and document discipline.

hard_gates.rationale: No complete mutation-sensitivity record was supplied for v1.3.
Key schema and semantic findings were observation-based.
The supplied comparison found complete manifest and metadata coverage.
v1.3 provided the stronger critical-schema regression floor.
Term, matrix, and SKOS representation was synchronized in the supplied comparison.
The baseline judgment was derived after the source comparison.

strengths: Complete inventory and metadata coverage in the supplied comparison
limitations: No complete adversarial validator suite was supplied
Some execution-governance and stale-record defects remained

metrics.rationale: v1.4 is mixed for Goal and requirement coverage.
v1.4 is mixed for Functional correctness.
v1.4 is mixed for Contract and schema integrity.

hard_gates.rationale: The validator accepted all seven representative severe mutations.
Work completion was not supported by target-grounded verification evidence.
Inventory and metadata authority did not fully cover the delivered tree.
Critical package invariants were not enforced by the release validator.
Term, concept-matrix, and SKOS surfaces were not synchronized.
The claimed PASS was inconsistent with defect sensitivity.

strengths: JSON-only control direction and tooling entrypoint

metrics.rationale: The v1.5 control pack is strong for Goal and objective precision.
The v1.5 control pack is strong for Baseline and change-set control.
The v1.5 control pack is strong for Scope and file obligations.
The v1.5 control pack is strong for Architecture and archetype fit.
The v1.5 control pack is strong for Verification design.
The v1.5 control pack is strong for Evidence contract.
The v1.5 control pack is strong for Drift safeguards.
The v1.5 control pack is strong for Oracle independence and defect sensitivity.
The v1.5 control pack is strong for Deliverable and acceptance clarity.

hard_gates.rationale: The PDD, ADR, and Reflection controls were readable and structured.
The control pack defined a governed change set, although some runtime details remained builder-selected.
The control language stated that scores should derive from gates.

strengths: Strong one-file work and JSON governance intent
Explicit generic Reflection and clean-extraction replay requirements
limitations: Exact replay implementation and broader-proof minimums were not fully frozen before execution
Independent audit mechanics were not sufficiently concrete

metrics.rationale: The v1.5 execution is weak for Authority acquisition.
The v1.5 execution is weak for Plan and work grounding.
The v1.5 execution is weak for Scope fidelity.
The v1.5 execution is weak for Execution traceability.
The v1.5 execution is weak for Evidence quality.
The v1.5 execution is weak for Test and validator quality.
The v1.5 execution is weak for Replay and final-artifact proof.
The v1.5 execution is weak for Correction-loop quality.
The v1.5 execution is weak for Verdict discipline.

hard_gates.rationale: The agent relied on a validator later shown to miss severe mutations.
Work verification evidence was chiefly assertion-only.
Downstream proof generation was not established as replay by the final package.
Runtime implementation details were selected during execution without a complete pre-scheduled file contract.
Zero blockers and PASS were claimed despite unresolved evidence and replay defects.

strengths: Kept proof ZIPs external to the glossary package

hard_gates.rationale: Five severe mutations were accepted.
Release claims depended on assertion-only evidence records.
The final package did not independently prove the downstream replay path.
I cannot confirm this. The supplied example does not reproduce every final hash and inventory check.
Critical schema strength regressed despite complete coverage claims.
Version, definition, and SKOS inconsistencies remained.

33 ADR records and one-target work records were present

claim_relations.rationale: v1.3 uses implemented schema validation, while v1.4 PASS remained insensitive to critical contract mutations.
The later package expanded semantic content without maintaining synchronization.
v1.4 added useful structural surfaces not central to the v1.3 claim.

drift_findings.observation: v1.4 added more explicit registry, project, generated, and tooling surfaces.
drift_findings.consequence: Potentially better navigation and future automation.

drift_findings.observation: Release-critical invariants were not detected by the validator.
drift_findings.consequence: The newer structure could certify broken states.

drift_findings.observation: New term content diverged from matrix and SKOS exports.
drift_findings.consequence: Human and machine semantic consumers received inconsistent meaning.

drift_findings.observation: Work records lost usable target grounding.
drift_findings.consequence: Completion could not be traced to verified file outcomes.

comparisons.conclusion: v1.4 is a structural donor, not the strongest primary baseline.

claim_relations.rationale: Both rely on package validation that remained mutation-insensitive.
Semantic synchronization claims persisted while inconsistencies remained.

drift_findings.observation: v1.5 added stronger formal controls and one-target work records.
drift_findings.consequence: The intended control model became clearer.

drift_findings.observation: Complete schema coverage was achieved with insufficiently strict critical schemas.
Proof artifacts were not established as replay from the final package.

comparisons.conclusion: Stronger controls did not translate into a stronger final package.

claim_relations.rationale: Strict governed evidence and contracts were reduced to shallow one-target PASS records.
The control required clean-extraction replay, while the agent sequence did not establish final-package replay.

drift_findings.observation: Strict schema intent became permissive implementation.
Evidence requirements became bulk PASS assertions.
Replay order and origin diverged from the control.
drift_findings.consequence: Downstream proof did not prove the final package runtime.

comparisons.conclusion: The decisive failure occurred between the strong control pack and its execution.

claim_relations.rationale: The no-blocker execution claim became the final PASS claim.
Shallow verification records and shallow schema contracts reinforced each other.

drift_findings.observation: The same false-PASS weakness persisted into the final result.
drift_findings.consequence: Packaging did not correct the execution oracle.

comparisons.conclusion: The result packaged the agent-work weaknesses rather than correcting them.

claim_relations.rationale: Strict release quality was claimed, but the result failed multiple hard gates.
Complete governance did not preserve meaningful schema strength.
Clean-extraction replay intent was not established by final validation.

drift_findings.observation: The result did not reach the strict dual-purpose release-quality goal.
drift_findings.consequence: The release is unsuitable as the next authoritative baseline.

comparisons.conclusion: v1.5 should be used as corrective input, not as the primary baseline.

problem_matches.observed_condition: Both validators accepted representative severe mutations.
problem_matches.consequence: The internal PASS signals were unreliable.

problem_matches.observed_condition: Nominal schema governance used permissive critical contracts.
corrective_actions: Restore strict required fields and item constraints

problem_matches.observed_condition: Work verification records asserted PASS without sufficient observations.
problem_matches.consequence: Completion and release claims were not independently reconstructable.
corrective_actions: Separate evidence capture from QA verdict

problem_matches.observed_condition: The builder created the artifact, validator result, evidence summary, and PASS.
problem_matches.consequence: Shared blind spots propagated into release acceptance.

problem_matches.observed_condition: The calculator fixture passed while unrelated package invariants remained broken.
problem_matches.consequence: The bounded fixture amplified false confidence.
corrective_actions: Add held-out proof and project-level negative tests

problem_matches.observed_condition: Proof generation was not established as replay from the final package.
corrective_actions: Run after final candidate creation from two clean extractions

problem_matches.observed_condition: Work records lacked concrete target grounding.
problem_matches.consequence: Completion counts could not be tied to verified outcomes.
corrective_actions: Rebuild the work plan with concrete targets and evidence links

problem_matches.consequence: Human and machine consumers received inconsistent meaning.

problem_matches.observed_condition: Old-version references remained active in v1.5.
problem_matches.consequence: Package authority and evidence attribution became ambiguous.
corrective_actions: Classify version references and repair active identity

problem_matches.observed_condition: PASS and zero blockers were recorded despite unresolved hard-gate defects.
corrective_actions: Reset verdict to pending and derive it after all gates

problem_matches.observed_condition: Newer versions were structurally richer but failed to preserve the strongest verified contracts.
problem_matches.consequence: Using the latest release as baseline would compound drift.
corrective_actions: Use only verified v1.4/v1.5 components as donors

problem_matches.observed_condition: Internal validation was not matched by an independent release oracle.
corrective_actions: Implement independent inventory, contract, evidence, and verdict checks

baseline_recommendation.rationale: v1.3 had the strongest implemented semantic, schema, inventory, and metadata contracts. v1.4 and v1.5 added useful structure and controls but failed release-critical hard gates.

donor_components.conditions: Migrate file by file and regenerate all authority and evidence from the v1.3 semantic core.

donor_components.component: generic Reflection control, one-target work shape, and approved ADR applicability model
donor_components.conditions: Reimplement with strict schemas, observation-bearing evidence, final-artifact replay, and independent audit.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Useful structure did not offset validator, work-plan, inventory, and semantic failures.
The stronger control pack was not faithfully implemented and the result failed multiple hard gates.

decisive_reasons: v1.3 preserves the strongest verified baseline contracts.
v1.4 improves structure but degrades defect sensitivity, work grounding, inventory, and semantic synchronization.
v1.5 control quality is substantially higher than its agent execution and final result.
The calculator fixture is not the root cause; its narrow PASS was generalized beyond its oracle.
priority_actions: Start the next build from v1.3 and import only verified donor components.
Freeze strict schemas, replay implementation, mutation oracles, and evidence fields before work planning.
Separate builder validation from independent release audit.
Generate proofs only from the final clean-extracted candidate.
Block PASS on semantic, contract, evidence, replay, inventory, or mutation hard-gate failure.
limitations: This example is intentionally abbreviated and is not a replacement for the full prior evaluation reports.
I cannot confirm this. The example package does not embed every original source artifact.
